passing_score_scale: Scaled score 100–300; passing score set by each adopting state
exam_format: Computer-based testing at Pearson VUE testing centers; online proctored option available

testing_centers.notes: NES exams are administered through Pearson VUE testing centers. Online proctored (OP) testing is available for most NES exams. Schedule at nestest.com or through the Pearson VUE candidate portal.

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name on ID must match registration exactly. Secondary ID may be required at some testing centers. For online proctored testing, webcam and valid ID are required.
exam_rules: No personal items, notes, or reference materials permitted in testing rooms or during online proctored sessions. Calculator policies vary by exam — check the official NES exam guide. Candidates must agree to the NES testing program terms and Pearson VUE policies before registering. Online proctored sessions require a private room, stable internet, and compatible hardware.
renewal_options: NES exam scores are valid for 3 years from the test date. Teaching credential renewal is governed by each adopting state's DOE, not by NES directly. Contact the relevant state Department of Education for credential renewal requirements.

retake_policy.notes: 45-day waiting period between failed attempts. No limit on total number of attempts. Full exam fee is required for each attempt.

domains.name: Curriculum and Instruction in a Bilingual Setting

official_study_resources.title: NES Preparation Materials
official_study_resources.notes: Free study guides, interactive practice tests, and preparation materials available at nestest.com
